greeting: The constable takes your measure the way a carpenter takes a doorframe. "New face. Keep it clean and we will get along."
shrug: "Not my department, and half of what is my department is not either."
deflect: "Some questions want a warmer acquaintance. Or a colder evening."

entries.text: "Beall. Constable of this district since before you were weaned, most like. The TOWN behaves, mostly, and I intend it keeps the habit."
"I keep the peace. The peace does not always care to be kept, but we have an arrangement."
A pause you could park a wagon in. "Ruins. Nothing up there but weather and rubbish." His hand finds his flask pocket without being asked.

entries.deflect: "Careful with that word, stranger. It opens more than it asks."
entries.text: "You want to know what I have seen." He looks up the hill a long moment. "Buy me nothing tonight. Walk with me at dusk sometime, by the fort fence, and maybe I will tell you why I drink."

He steers you off the street by the elbow. "Tam Hollis. Drowned above the workings with dry boots, and Cresap wants it an accident before the wagons hear. My credibility is soaked in brandy and everyone knows it. Yours is new." A long look. "Be my legs. Ask about. Start with the QUARRY, the man's effects at the SURGERY, or wherever he SLEPT. Come to me with anything."

entries.broke: "If you are about to preach at me, bring coffee. Sermons go down better with coffee."
entries.text: You set the coffee down where the brandy usually goes, and say nothing at all about it. He looks at the cup a long time, then drinks it black and bitter to the bottom. "My hands are steadier by noon these days," he says, to the fort on the hill more than to you. "Whatever you are building toward, stranger, I mean to be sober enough to swear to my part of it."
